back_translation: After Joshua died, the descendants of Israel inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Which tribe of us will go up to fight against the Canaanites as the first?'
The LORD said, 'Judah shall go up. Behold, I have delivered the land into his hand.'

Then Judah said to Simeon his brother, 'Go up with us in the territory allotted to us, and let us fight against the Canaanites together, and we will go with you in your territory as well.' So Simeon went with him.
When Judah went up, the LORD delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand, and they struck down ten thousand men at Bezek.
There they found Adoni-Bezek and fought against him, and struck down the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
Adoni-Bezek fled, and they pursued him, seized him, and cut off the thumbs of his hands and the big toes of his feet.

Adoni-Bezek said, 'Seventy kings, with the thumbs of their hands and the big toes of their feet cut off, used to gather the scraps of food under my table. As I did to them, so God has repaid me.' Then they brought him to Jerusalem, and he died there.
Then the descendants of Judah fought against Jerusalem and captured it; they struck down the inhabitants with the edge of the sword and burned the city with fire.
After that, the descendants of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites who lived in the hill country, in the Negev, and in the foothills.
Judah went up against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron (formerly Hebron was called Kiriath-arba), and they struck down Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.
From there he went to fight against the inhabitants of Debir (formerly Debir was called Kiriath-sepher).
Caleb proclaimed, 'Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give my daughter Acsah to him as wife.'
Othniel son of Kenaz, the younger brother of Caleb, captured the city, so Caleb gave his daughter Acsah to him as wife.
When she arrived, she urged him to ask her father for a piece of land, and she got down from her donkey. Caleb asked, 'What do you want?'
She answered, 'Please bless me, since you have given me land in the Negev, give me springs of water as well.' So Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

The descendants of the Kenite, the father-in-law of Moses, went up from the City of Palms together with the descendants of Judah, to the wilderness of Judah in the Negev near Arad, and they went to dwell among the people.
Judah went with Simeon his brother, and they struck down the Canaanites who lived in Zephath, and destroyed the city completely. They called the name of that city Hormah.
Judah also captured Gaza, Ashkelon, and Ekron — each city with its territory.
The LORD was with Judah, and they took possession of the hill country, but they could not drive out the inhabitants of the plains because those had iron chariots.
They gave Hebron to Caleb just as Moses had promised, and Caleb drove out the three sons of Anak from there.
As for the Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem, the descendants of Benjamin did not drive them out. So the Jebusites still live with the descendants of Benjamin in Jerusalem to this day.
The house of Joseph also went up to fight against Bethel, and the LORD was with them.
The house of Joseph sent men to spy out Bethel (formerly this city was called Luz).
The spies saw a man coming out of the city and said to him, 'Please show us the entrance to the city, and we will treat you with kindness.'
The man showed them the entrance to the city, and they struck the city with the edge of the sword, but released that man and all his family.
The man went to the land of the Hittites, built a city, and named the city Luz, which is its name to this day.
Manasseh did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shean, Taanach, Dor, Ibleam, Megiddo, and the villages surrounding those cities, because the Canaanites were determined to dwell in that land.
When Israel grew strong, they pressed the Canaanites into forced labor, but did not drive them out completely.
Ephraim also did not drive out the Canaanites who lived in Gezer; so the Canaanites lived in Gezer among them.
Zebulun also did not drive out the inhabitants of Kitron and Nahalol; so the Canaanites lived among them and became forced laborers.
Asher also did not drive out the inhabitants of Acco, of Sidon, of Ahlab, of Achzib, of Helbah, of Aphik, and of Rehob.
So the Asherites lived among the Canaanites who were the inhabitants of the land, because they did not drive them out.

Naphtali also did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and of Beth-anath. So the Naphtalites lived among the Canaanites who were the inhabitants of the land, but the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and of Beth-anath became their forced laborers.
The Amorites pressed the descendants of Dan into the hill country, and did not allow them to come down into the valley.
The Amorites were determined to dwell at Mount Heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim, but when the house of Joseph grew strong, they pressed the Amorites into forced labor.
The territory of the Amorites extended from the Ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upward.
